# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { URL } from 'url';
import { env } from './config';

const moduleName = process.env.MODULE_NAME || new URL(window.location.search).searchParams.get('module');
if (!moduleName) {
  throw new Error("No module name specified");
}

const modulePath = `./${moduleName}.ts`;

dynamicImport(modulePath)
  .then((module) => {
    const customFunction = module.default as () => void;
    return customFunction();
  })
  .catch((error) => {
    console.error('Error loading module', error);
    process.exit(1);
  });

function dynamicImport(path: string): Promise<any> {
  return import(path);
}
